title: Data Validation Lower Cased Formula when referencing another sheet
author: ralphchristianeclipse

comments.author: raro28
comments.body:
I am having the same problem in formulas like
=PRODUCT($charges.H2,1/100)

it gets transformed to =PRODUCT($charges.h2,1/100)

comments.author: Aelexe
comments.body: I am also having this issue.

comments.author: Aelexe
comments.body:
After a brief dive into the code I found it works fine for my scenario if I use ! instead of . as the separator.

So `data!$A$2:$A$8` instead of `data.$A$2:$A$8`.
